task_completion.description: Verify the Electrical Engineering Calculations app is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main window of the Electrical Engineering Calculations app should be visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify the Ohm's Law Current Calculator is selected or active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The specific tool or tab for 'Ohm's Law Current Calculator' (or similar wording for calculating current) should be clearly selected or displayed within the app.

task_completion.description: Verify the voltage value is entered correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The number '12' should be visible inside the Voltage input field of the calculator.

task_completion.description: Verify the resistance value is entered correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The number '4' should be visible inside the Resistance input field of the calculator.

task_completion.description: Verify the current has been calculated and the correct result is displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The calculated result '3' (or '3 A') should be displayed in the result area or Current output field of the calculator.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the application's graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show interaction with the Electrical Engineering Calculations app GUI, without terminal windows or command-line scripts being used to bypass the app.

integrity.description: Verify the task was not spoofed using a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The inputs and results must be located within the specific UI elements of the calculator app, not simply typed out in Notepad, a text editor, or a web browser.

integrity.description: Verify the result appears as a genuine computed output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The result '3' should appear in a read-only result field, a popup, or a designated output area of the app, rather than being manually typed by the agent into an editable text box.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must enter a Voltage of 12 and a Resistance of 4 into the Ohm's Law Current Calculator. The expected calculated Current result is exactly 3 (or 3 A).
